label: notion-redesigned
note: Workflow-level redesign of the official Notion MCP server's 24 tools. Same capability surface: search, read/write pages (markdown in/out instead of block trees), query/manage databases, comments, users. The server translates markdown to Notion blocks internally — which the official server already does for its two markdown tools.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search Notion by title or content. Returns matching pages and databases with id, title, type, and parent.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous call

tools.name: read_page
tools.description: Read a page's content as markdown, including its properties. Use the page id from search results.

include_comments.description: Also return the page's comments (default false)

tools.name: write_page
tools.description: Create a new page or update an existing one using markdown content. To create, pass parent_id and title. To update, pass page_id and one of: content (replaces body), append (adds to end), or properties.

parent_id.description: Parent page or database id (required when creating)

append.description: Markdown to append to the end of the page

properties.description: Page property values keyed by property name

tools.name: query_database
tools.description: Query a Notion database. Returns rows as flat objects keyed by property name. Filter syntax: {property, operator, value}, e.g. {"property": "Status", "operator": "equals", "value": "Done"}. Combine with and/or arrays.

tools.name: manage_database
tools.description: Create a database or update its schema. Properties map property names to types: title, rich_text, number, select, multi_select, date, people, checkbox, url, email, relation.

properties.description: Property name → type (or {type, options} for selects)

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a page or reply to a discussion thread.

discussion_id.description: Thread to reply to (instead of page_id)

tools.name: get_users
tools.description: List workspace users, or get one user by id. Returns id, name, email, and type (person/bot).
